source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A mass moves along the $x$-axis following the position vs. time graph provided. During what time intervals does the mass have an acceleration of $0 \frac{\text{m}}{\text{s}^{2}}$?
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object string representation rather than a file path or URL

issues: metadata.image_paths contains a PIL image object string representation rather than a file path or URL

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says a position-vs-time graph is provided
determining acceleration intervals depends on reading visual graph structure rather than text alone
multi_step: solver must infer that acceleration is zero when velocity is constant on a position-time graph
solver then must identify the time intervals on the graph that match this condition
answer contains two distinct intervals, indicating interval extraction rather than a single direct lookup
verifiability: answer_text gives explicit time intervals that can be checked against the graph
answer_type is text but the expected form is constrained and specific
registry_passed=true and both question and answer are present in the registry

risk_flags: metadata.image_paths contains a PIL image object string representation rather than a file path or URL
